entry.title: Re: Ariba Guided Buying : PO creation in quality environment
entry.content:
in standard without customizations you cannot be connected to two ERP systems from one Ariba realm/system.

so you point interface towards quality or dev that's it.

if needed - it is possible to purchase a DEV realm if needed for additional price from SAP

entry.title: What’s New in DTV 1.6
entry.content:
DTV 1.6 is released and in this blog, you will find the key features regarding DTV 1.6. Following are the key features of DTV 1.6.

Pre-Delivered Content: Enhanced with additional reports

DTV 1.6 now supports 60+ SAP reports and transactions. Following reports are now newly added in the latest pre-delivered content.

The latest pre-delivered content file is DTV_content_V3.1.dtv

Check pre-delivered content Note for more information: 3258383 - DTV : SAP Pre- delivered Content

Custom namespace reports with ALV output are allowed to be validated within the tool

Starting DTV 1.6 release, along with Custom reports (Z&Y), the tool can also validate custom namespace reports. This feature has been an ask from many of our customers and with this release now custom namespace reports with ALV output types can be validated.

Integration of DTV with Business Transformation Cockpit to support Lean SDT

Along with System conversion and SAP S/4HANA updates/upgrades, DTV can also be used in case of Selective Data Transition (SDT) scenarios with its latest release DTV 1.6.

Performance improvements in Simulation, Extraction and Evaluation

In DTV 1.6 performance of each execution (Simulation, Extraction and Evaluation) is improved making the overall DTV execution faster.

Evaluation Summary screen is revamped with additional fields

Evaluation Summary screen is revamped with new additional fields such as Report Name, Report Description, Runtime, Overall Runtime, Overall Delay, Workitem runtime, Workitem Delay etc. which are useful for evaluation.

DTV related useful links and Notes

  • SAP Note 3117879 – Central SAP Note for DTV
  • SAP Note 3569864 – DTV Tool 1.6 Delivery
  • Help Documentation – Data Transition Validation
  • Business data validation using DTV tool - Blog post
  • Best practices to follow during DTV implementation
  • Work Item Level Execution in DTV

DTV 1.6 Bug Fixing Note

3581960 - DTV 1.6 - Bug Fixes

3584455 - Evaluation Bug Fix for RFBELJ00

yes 50 is the current intended limitation.

entry.title: Re: Can Ariba send POs to Non-enabled suppliers via Email...
entry.content:
technically if supplier does not register on Network and not take ownership of the account then you can consider such supplier a non-enabled one.. account is there on Network but nobody has any ownership of it.

these suppliers will keep receiving Purchase Orders to their email

entry.title: Re: CPI iFlow getting error: The metadata constraints '[P...
entry.content:
Hi @patrik_buransky ,

When testing, try without the PostingDate field as it's optional, or alternatively, include the PostingDate as '2025-03-25T00:00:00'. Both options should be valid solutions.
Additionally, I've observed that the PostingDate is being sent as '17423067770', which corresponds to 'Tue Jul 21 1970 15:44:27.770'. Ideally, it should be sent as '1742306777000', which represents 'Tue Mar 18 2025 14:06:17.000'.

Kind regards. Vijay

entry.title: Re: Performance based equipment task list with multiple intervals
entry.content:
Greetings,

maintenance strategies (IP11) are very flexible and you can have many different maintenance packages included. Furthermore you can include hierarchy in the packages so one takes precedence over the other. You can then use this maintenance strategy for multiple pieces of equipment.

Can you elaborate a bit further regarding "There are only 2 digits to display the interval of the maintenance plan." --> can you share a screenshot of your issue here.

Bets regards, M.

entry.title: Calculation View Features of 2025 QRC1
entry.content:
Within the time frame of 2025 Q1, several new calculation view features have been released in SAP Business Application Studio when connected to SAP HANA Cloud database QRC1. Some of these features are highlighted below. You can find examples that illustrate the individual features here. An overview of features of other releases can be found here.

Variables of Input Type Derived

With the new variable input type "Derived" filters can be constructed based on values that are returned in a tabular structure.

For example, a table function can serve as the source of all values that should be included in a filter. In the following screenshot a filter is constructed based on the returned values in column "filterValues" (2.) of tabular data source "filterTableNV" (1.). The filter is applied on column "city" (3.).

The resulting filter string could look like

 

"city" IN ( SELECT "filterValues" FROM "filterTableNV" )

 

 

The construction of the filter is the responsibility of the consuming analytics client and the user for executing the query requires privilege SELECT or EXECUTE for the tabular data source.
 
Use this option to flexibly construct filters at query execution time.
 

Copy Calculated/Restricted Columns and Input Parameters/Variables between Calculation Views

Calculated and restricted columns can be copied between different calculation views as long as the target node type matches the source node type.
Similarly, input parameters and variables can be copied.
 
If dependencies, such as used columns are missing in the target node a warning is shown and the dependency removed. If the dependency is mandatory the element has to be manually adapted.
 
More easily reuse your modelling developments across calculation views with this feature.
 

Clean Up Intermediate Data Preview Objects

Per default after using the intermediate data preview object option temporary objects for the intermediate data preview remain until the calculation view is redeployed. These objects can also be removed using the clean up option that is available during Data Preview With Other Database User:

Within this dialog the Clean Up option becomes available that allows to selectively remove remaining objects in the selected schema.

Remaining intermediate data preview objects prior to the database upgrade cannot be removed in this way and need a redeployment.

Remove remaining intermediate data preview objects after e.g., firefighting sessions

Choose Target Feature Version of a Project

Select which features should be available in the modeler for a given project. Only versions between the last frozen feature version and the current database release can be selected as target feature version. Selecting a feature version freezes it.

Use this option to keep available modeling features e.g., in development systems in sync with productive systems.

Support of Vector Engine Functions in Expressions

Vector engine functions such as VECTOR_EMBEDDING can now be used in expressions. VECTOR_EMBEDDING transforms data into a multidimensional vector based on a given model. Usage of the functionality requires the enablement of Natural Language Processing in the respective account.

Leverage the strength of HANA vector engine in expressions

Additional Execution Hints

With execution hint "conversion_result_type_downcast_after_aggregation" downcasting of currency converted measures to their target data type can be postponed until after the aggregation of the currency converted measure. This can be used to decrease rounding errors and to reduce resource consumption.

 

Execution hint "HIERARCHY_REMOVE_DUPLICATE_EDGES" controls whether duplicate edges that link a child node to the same parent node twice in a hierarchy are removed or not.

 
HIERARCHY_REMOVE_DUPLICATE_EDGES overwrites the default behavior. If set to TRUE also in case of shared hierarchies multiple edges are removed. If set to FALSE also in case of local hierarchies no removal of duplicate edges happens.
 
Increase control over calculation view execution by using hints if needed

entry.title: Re: Can BAPI_MTRREADDOC_UPLOAD create specific meter reading orders depending on registers?
entry.content:
Good Morning UD39,

I guess what Ricky means is, that it shall not be forgotten to pass the info of MRREASON within the structure BAPIEABLU as the info of meter reading reason is mandatory in the EABLG table. So this makes this field also mandatory in the BAPI usage.

Kind Regards

entry.title: Subcontracting with a sales order stock or a normal stock?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a question regarding subcontracting, In standard subcontracting with MTO with production.

When sending a material to a subcontractor, do we use a standard stock or  a sales order stock , in my case we are sending a semi-finished material, and in that case, how the consumption is happening at the subcontractor, that is totally based on which stock we are sending, also please note that we are using configurable material.

My confusion is how the consumption is done if we are sending a sales order stock, do we need to create a specific storage location for each subcontractor?

entry.title: Re: How to Call Web Service BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkRepli...
entry.content:
Hello, 

1.)

how to modify Webservice note during CR Rejected. 

With the above ask I guess you want to alter the contents of Note property which we have under Log node/entity of BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkReplicateConfirmation_Out , if yes then utilize the below BADI's Method which gets triggered when the BP is successfully replicated and the Confirmation is sent outwards to the caller system.

BADI: MDG_SE_BP_BULK_REPLRQ_IN

Method: IF_MDG_SE_BP_BULK_REPLRQ_IN~OUTBOUND_PROCESSING

the above Structure has the LOG node in that.

2.) 

So, how to call this BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkReplicateConfirmation_Out Web service in System Caller Badi

ABAP Class "CO_MDG_BP_RPLCTCO" is the Internal ABAP Class(SE24) artefact for this WebService,

So try calling the same as highlighted below using "LOGICAL_PORT" you would have configured in the SOAMANAGER,

*DATA xxxxxxxx TYPE REF TO CO_MDG_BP_RPLCTCO.

TRY.
CREATE OBJECT xxxxxxxx
* EXPORTING
* destination =
* logical_port_name =
.
CATCH cx_ai_system_fault .
ENDTRY.

*TRY.
CALL METHOD xxxxxxxx->bp_suitebulk_replct_conf
EXPORTING
output =
.
* CATCH cx_ai_system_fault.
*ENDTRY.

Used the below option of ABAP Editor "Pattern" for getting the above code,

Give the above solutions a try.

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Re: Integration Financial Plan in PPM and SAC Planning
entry.content:
Ok, got it. Did you have a look at the content package for Portfolio Financial Planning? 

This will only cover the financial planning part of portfolio management and facilitate a alignment of the top-down/bottom-up planning. Values are planned in SAC and then transferred either directly to the top level WBS element of the PS project to S/4HANA or for further detailing the approved value to WBS elements, you can also leverage the models of the Integrated Financial Planning and then transfer the detailed values to S/4. With the import from S/4 the actual and plan cost from PS are being retrieved and visualized in the planning.

The package covers an example for a very lightweight planning process leveraging already existing PS projects. Things which you get out of portfolio management like support of the request phase in project where you do not yet have a PS project, structured decision gates, trigger of PS project creation etc. are not covered here. 

The integration resides purely on ACDOCP/A. This also means that there is no budget availability control for projects based on the planning. These values can only be used for comparison. If you want to leverage them for availability control, they would have to be copied over to the classic Budget. 

Hope this helps, you can also DM if you want to discuss in more detail. Andrea 

Since the transfer is ACDOCP

entry.title: Re: Group Report Doesn't Read Doc Type EE
entry.content:
Difficult to speculate based on the provided information. Normally data posted in general ledger accounting should be integrated with the release task to Group Reporting independent from accounting document types used.

Did you check: 

- ledger -> version assignment

- company code -> company ID -> consolidation unit

- GL account -> FS item assignment

entry.title: CAP messaging with IS Event Mesh
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I'm trying to connect CAP with Integration Suite Event Mesh (not advanced event mesh). https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/messaging#event-mesh-shared 

BTP Event Mesh and IS Advanced Event Mesh are working out of the box for sending and receiving messages. However the "normal" IS Event Mesh will only send messages.

When configuring a receiver it failes, because by default it tries to create the queue it connects to.

Looking in the file "/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/messaging/AMQPWebhookMessaging.js" there is actually a check on a "doNotDeploy" property, but it isn't passed along. What is the reason this isn't implemented? Just passing the options along, makes it work by adding the "doNotDeploy" to the package.json.

Any additional information to understanding why would be greatly appreciated 🙂 

/D

entry.title: Re: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
entry.content:
hi nagesh , 

Thanks for the detailed blog.

i have a question

Background – We already have joule enable for our SuccessFactors ( global account (A) subaccount - A1) which is up and running .

We want to enable joule for our public cloud to leverage its capabilities. Please note our public cloud resides in another global account ( B )

 

Applicationglobal accountsubaccount
successfactorAA1
Public cloudBB1

Our query is that can we leverage the existing joule (SuccessFactors subaccount) or we have to run the booster on our public cloud global account(B).

Thanks in advance

@nageshcaparthy

i propose: Start OSS dialog. This is the best option to clarify your topic.

C.B.

entry.title: Re: Inheritance of responsible cost center in project bui...
entry.content:
Hello Mohammed, 

in my opinion you can easily go with a substitution for the wbs-elements. 

1. Tcode: OPSN | Create a new Substitution-Folder and a new Substitution-Step for wbs-element

2. Precondition should be: PRPS-STUFE (wbs-element level below your top-level-wbs-element) >= e.g. '2' 

3. Substitution: Your field PRPS-FKSTL is substituted by field RCJ_PRPSUP-FKSTL

3. Tcode: OPSA - Select the Substitution-folder name in the project profile and select the checkbox "autom. substitute at save" 

4. You have the configure the field control for your cost center field, that it is only mandatory for the top-level wbs-element (influenced by PRPS-STUFE)

Please correct me if I am wrong. 

I hope this helps. 

Best regards, 

Tobias

entry.title: Re: Error program EHS_WWI LABEL not registered
entry.content: Hello: was there any change on WWI level on this WWI server? a new WWI patch? If wwi is up and running: normally no such problem comes up. But e.g. Windows OS System change and othere reasons can change this. As mentioned by JuanCarlos: try to understand the System log Wish you the best C.B.

entry.title: Hello we have problems with Office Key user in CG54
entry.content:
Dear all

there is a system set up problem with SAP user and "office key" as assigned to the SAP user. And because of this the user can not find his/her "OfficeKey" number in transaction CG54.

There seems to exist several "correction" reports as delivered by SAP (i have found i believe three different variants). But we do not know: which of the several ABAP Routines will help.

E.g. problem is like: by using the "Name" you can not find the corresponding "SO key (in CG54) But by using SE16 you find the data (and chekcing the relevant SAP tables) and if you use the SO key as identified: You can use this to dispatch the SDS

We would like to ge rid of this problem. Seems to be a "system set up" problem.  Any idea?

C.B.

entry.title: Product view in APO does not display the transaction data for component supplied by receiving plant
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are having subcontracting activity in our set up. I am getting in the this scenario.

Entities: PLant - PT1, Vendor - VN1, FG - MAT1[subcontracting material], Component - CMP1.

Heuristics executed for MAT1 at PT1. Pur req generated to VN1. In the ECC system, 'Components' on 'Material Data' tab page of the Pur Req displays the compnent CMP1 to be supplied by PT1 to VN1. But same is not displayed on the Prodcut view for CMP1 - PT1 in APO system. Can some one help to get the details on CMP1 - PT1 transaction in the APO system?

Thanks & Regards

Piyush Ranpura

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello,

I am not sure if I understand correctly? Do you want to print your production orders via batch jobs?
You can link the printing to your production order release event (Output parameter determination)
You can manually "Mass processing Production orders", but you cannot schedule this as a job. You have to run it manually.

If you really need to automate it, you could use a API to trigger the releaset from outside.

I hope this helps.
Regards Johannes

entry.title: Re: SAP Marketing Cloud Release 2402 is Here!
entry.content:
Hi @vervinckt_joyca ,

The utm parameters should be automatically stored on the interaction that you have in the form.

We just recently implemented some custom logic to copy the utm paramaters from the form fillout interaction into the lead that is triggered by the form fillout.

Kind Regards,

Markus

entry.title: Re: Functional area derived from cost center category?
entry.content:
Hello Sen,

Thank you for your response! I now understand that functional areas can be derived from multiple sources, including cost center categories, G/L accounts, manual journal entries, and cost center master data.

I have a follow-up question: If I explicitly enter a functional area in the migration template, will it override the one derived from the cost center category? Or does SAP follow a specific priority hierarchy when determining the final functional area assignment?

I’d really appreciate any insights on this to ensure correct data migration.

Looking forward to your response.

Hi Singh,

Could you check if you enable "Use in Key User Apps"?

Best Regards,

Zhehui

entry.title: Re: How to update a boolean field type using determinatio...
entry.content: {extensions/NDAcheck} = ( true ) this syntax works for the boolean, please let me know if it worked for you

entry.title: Word macros for Translation file
entry.content:
Hi Community,

Our author encountering this notice in producer while importing translation.docx file. This is a recurring issue since last year. 

While doing internal investigation, I came across the following:-

1. File path: When we export the translation file it was downloaded by default in docx format to our machine. But the document type change to dotm during uploading. Not sure if this is something to have a closer look that causes the error.

2. It appears the Macros in the document was not allowed in our organization. We have global policy which cannot be changed. 

While I hope to find a solution (perhaps it is available), for one of the option below. Preferably, option 1.

Option 1. Is there a translation file template without the need of macros and working as expected? Due to the translation accuracy, our authors and users has chosen this manual option compared to machine translation.

In addition, I have looked up Enable or disable macros in Microsoft 365 files,

Option 2. To have these 'macros' digitally signed in the Trust Center.

In order to do so, we have to obtain the new Trusted Publishers certificate SAP SE and periodically upload the certificate manually by each author in their machine. we have limited administration rights in our machine this may not be a long-term solution.

Much appreciate your help here.

entry.title: 管理対象システムのDB認証設定について
entry.content:
Solution Managerの管理対象システム設定の「Enter System Parameters」の「DB Parameters」設定についてのご質問になります。
管理対象システムがABAPシステムの場合は、Solution ManagerからのDB接続を可能にするために、SQLServerの認証設定をインストールガイド記載の
「Windows認証のみ」の設定から「Windows認証とSQL認証」に変更する必要があるかと思いますが、その変更によって問題が発生することはないとの理解でよろしいでしょうか？
例えば、パフォーマンスやセキュリティの観点等で問題が発生することはないとの理解でよろしいでしょうか？

[English]
To connect database SQLServer to Solution Manager for Managed System Configuration, according to guide, it is required to change authentication from "Windows Authentication only" to "SQL Server and Windows Authentication mode".
Will this change cause any problem? e.g. performance problem or security problem etc.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 管理対象システムのDB認証設定について
entry.content:
Solution Manager監視対象システム設定「Enter System Parameters」の「DB Parameters」について、下記関連ノートをご参照ください。
1265134 - DBA Cockpit: Connection of a remote database
1458291 - SolMan 7.1 and higher - Database Warehouse & Alerting for MSSQL
1388700 - SolMan 7.0 EHP1 Database Warehouse for MSSQL
1316740 - Set up remote monitoring for Microsoft SQL Server databases


認証設定、パフォーマンスとセキュリティ関連ノート抜粋：
[1388700]
SQL Server ログインの登録

        2.最初に、SQL Server が SQL Server 認証用に設定されていることを確認してください。  サーバを右クリックして [プロパティ] を選択し、[セキュリティ] をクリックします。  "SQL Server および Windows 認証モード" を選択します。OK をクリックします。


[1316740]
Solution Manager を使用して接続を設定しない場合は、DBACockpit でマニュアルで設定することができます。これを行う場合は、対象システムに SQL Server sysadmin ログインを使用してこの接続を登録することに注意してください。SAP_BASIS 700、701、710、711、および 720 を基盤とする NetWeaver リリースでは、リモートシステムの監視にこの sysadmin ログインが使用されます。 つまり、DBA コックピットで SAP ユーザに対象システムに対する全権限を付与すると、SAP ユーザは対象システムの任意のデータを読込、変更、または削除することができます。非常に安全な SAP インストール環境において、またはクリティカルなパフォーマンス状況によりリモートモニタを即時に一時的に接続する必要がある場合にのみ、この方法でマニュアルで DBACockpit を設定することをお奨めします。


English
Regarding Solution Manager Managed System Configuration step Enter System Parameters, DB parameters, please refer to following notes for reference:
1265134 - DBA Cockpit: Connection of a remote database
1458291 - SolMan 7.1 and higher - Database Warehouse & Alerting for MSSQL
1388700 - SolMan 7.0 EHP1 Database Warehouse for MSSQL
1316740 - Set up remote monitoring for Microsoft SQL Server databases

Below note extractions had mentioned authentication, performance and security(login)
[1388700]
Creating a SQL Server login.

    2. First make sure the SQL Server is configured for SQL Server authentication.  Right-click on the server, choose Properties and then click on Security.  Choose "SQL Server and Windows Authentication mode". Click OK.

[1316740]
If you do not use Solution Manager to setup the connection, you can do so manually in DBACockpit. If you do this, then be aware that you will create this connection using a SQL Server sysadmin login to the target system. In NetWeaver releases based on SAP_BASIS 700, 701, 710, 711 and 720 this sysadmin login will be used to monitor the remote system.  This means that if you give an SAP user full authorizations to the target system in DBACockpit then that SAP user could read, change or delete ANY data in the target system. We only recommend manually configuring DBACockpit this way in extremely secure SAP installation environments or when you need to connect a remote monitor immediately and temporarily due to critical performance situations.

Best Regards,
David Song

entry.title: Systems entry in Landscape of Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We have our systems(SAP,HDB and BTP,SAC etc) on SAP RISE and SAP ECS/HEC are supporting my SAP infrastructure.
Recently, we have onboarded cloud ALM in landscape and tried to integrate S/4 systems(using t. Code /sdf/alm_setup).

But when I check in Cloud ALM Landscape management, I already see entries of all the systems.
I just wanted to know how systems are synced/added to cloud ALM automatically without added using /sdf/alm_setup.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: EAM_WORKFLOW_AGENTS_BADI | Usage of MaintenanceOrderData
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm working on creating a custom workflow for Maintenance Orders in SAP S4/Hana public cloud on 2SL with CBC. Due to the environment, I have limited to no ability to debug code.

I aim to use the controllingsettlementprofile from the CDS View I_maintenanceorderdex to guide who gets to approve the Maintenance Order. For instance, when the WBS is set, I would like the project manager to be the approver.

However, I'm facing the following issues:

DATA:
  ls_badi_approver TYPE if_eam_workflow_agents_badi=>bd_eam_s_previous_approver,
  lt_badi_approver TYPE if_eam_workflow_agents_badi=>bd_eam_t_previous_approver,
  lv_projectmanager      TYPE YY1_Projectmanager-personfullname,
  lv_cb_id TYPE i_businessuserbasic-userid,
  lv_coprofile TYPE i_maintenanceorderdex.

IF businessobject CS 'MaintenanceOrder'.
  "DATA(lv_level) = lines( previousapproverlist ).

  " Holen der Maintenance Order-Daten
 SELECT SINGLE *
  FROM I_MaintenanceOrderDEX WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
  INTO _coprofile.

  IF SY-SUBRC = 0.

    "IF lv_coprofile-controllingsettlementprofile EQ 'YEAM06'.

    "When PSP is set use the psp
    IF lv_coprofile-projectinternalid IS NOT INITIAL.

I'm encountering an issue where every project-related ID (such as wbselementid, projectinternalid, etc.) from the CDS View appears empty, even though they are filled. Additionally, the SettlementProfile is also empty during code execution. I verified this by setting a dummy approver at each step.

I receive Maintenance Orders where the equipment details are provided, but I'm unable to obtain the rest of the necessary information.

Could anyone offer guidance on this issue?

Your assistance would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,
Benjamin

entry.title: Re: Systems entry in Landscape of Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

The Private Cloud Service/system in the ECS environment will be imported automatically to the SAP Cloud ALM by the daily import job.
Please review the guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/lms-connecting-services.
That is why you can see the system with type "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition". By registering the system with /sdf/alm_setup will register the client to relevant system.

However, currently there is some known issue with this, please review the KB for more details.

0003582009 Changes to the product registration of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in SAP Cloud ALM is causing multiple issues

Best regards,
Peng

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG Validation Rules: New BRF+ expression types supported for Export/Import
entry.content:
Dear All,

An updated from my side. There is actually a note already in place - https://me.sap.com/notes/3340846.

And it resolves the issue, I have tested in a system with the newest release and it works all fine.

 

Kind Regards,

Vasil

entry.title: Re: MTA DB deployments fail with error: Cannot read prope...
entry.content:
Hi rbanania,

I had the exact same issue and how I managed to fix it was by deleting any .csv files that I had. 
Hope this helps someone.

Regards,
Valentin

entry.title: Discussion forum no longer moderated
entry.content:
Dear Learners,

We hope you enjoyed the “Getting Started with Terraform on SAP BTP” course.

The discussion forum for this course will no longer be moderated. We thank everyone for submitting their questions, suggestions, and feedback.

As mentioned at the start of the course, the following channels will remain open for you to provide feedback:

  • You can provide feedback on lesson level using the smileys below the videos in the course. 
  • For technical and general questions, use the Feedback function in the top left corner of the learning page.

If you have questions regarding the exercise material provided on GitHub, please address them in the GitHub Discussions.

See you in the next SAP Learning MOOC.

Thank you for choosing SAP Learning!

Kind regards,
Britta Radicke-Schlinsog
(SAP Learning team)

entry.title: AI-Generated User Feedback Summary in Customer Usage Dashboard
entry.content:
Introducing User Feedback in SAP for Me: Empowering Customer Admins to Improve User Experience 

The Customer Usage Dashboard in SAP for Me is an essential tool that helps organizations monitor their usage and adoption of SAP products, as mentioned in a previous blog post. With our latest launch, we've added a new AI feature to display end-user feedback. This gives the customer admins visibility into user challenges that can be resolved on the customer side to close the feedback loop. Currently piloted with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, this feature helps IT admins proactively address user concerns for a smoother adoption and better user experience. 

How it works 

We collect user feedback in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition via in-product surveys. You can read more about the data collection process here or in this blog post. There are two types of feedback given by the users in the free text field of in-product surveys: 1) feedback that SAP can take action on (i.e. feature related), and 2) user concerns that can be addressed on the customer side (i.e. implementation and onboarding related). By leveraging AI, we trained the system to filter and present relevant feedback, allowing customers to take proactive actions to improve their employees onboarding experience.  

By integrating feedback insights into SAP for Me, we are enabling customer admins to: 

  • Gain visibility into real user challenges that can be addressed on their side 
  • Resolve issues faster, reducing user frustration and support tickets 
  • Improve onboarding processes, ensuring users can efficiently adopt the product at no additional cost 
  • Close the feedback loop, motivating both users and customer admins to engage more actively 

Where to find the feature

On the usage tab, the “Product Experience” section is on the bottom of the page. You can read textual feedback summaries to understand user challenges. You can also export the feedback summary into excel by clicking “Export to Excel” button. 

Possible scenarios & how to interpret them 

Scenario 1: “Product Experience” section is available with feedback summary 

What it means: Users are actively providing textual feedback and there are at least 7 actionable user feedback comments available. The feedback summary is generated and displayed. 

How to improve: Look for common patterns in the feedback and take action to address them. 

Scenario 2: The “Nothing here, sorry!” message display 

What it means: When there are less than 7 actionable user feedback comments available, the data is not enough to generate a feedback summary. A low number of comments may also suggest fewer issues. 

How to improve: Encourage end-users to provide feedback by promoting feedback features within onboarding communications.  

Scenario 3: “Product Experience” section is hidden 

What it means: Your company, country, or region currently do not allow users to provide feedback in the system. 

How to improve: Contact SAP support for region-specific availability. Meanwhile, continue gathering user experience insights through internal feedback mechanisms. 

Final thoughts & next steps 

The introduction of User Feedback in SAP for Me marks a significant step forward in bridging the gap between user experience insights and customer administrators’ ability to take action. By providing a closed-loop feedback system, this feature ensures that customer admins can proactively improve user adoption, enhance onboarding experiences, and ultimately drive better business outcomes. 

Start exploring User Feedback in SAP for Me today. For more details, please reach out to your SAP account contact to have a discussion. We also recommend raising awareness about the feedback functionality among end users to encourage participation and gather more valuable insights. 

Related Blogs 

SAP Customer Usage Dashboard: 

  • Customer Usage Dashboard 2nd Edition for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Do you want to see how your organization is using SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition? 

User Feedback: 

  • Continuous Improvements Continue with Your Feedback 
  • Your feedback helps to improve SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • With highest Appreciation! Your Experience Feedback helps us improve Your Products 
  • An Uber for Engineering? 

Learn More 

  • SAP for Me @ sap.com 
  • SAP for Me @ SAP Community 
  • Sign in to SAP for Me 
  • SAP ONE Support Launchpad Transition to SAP for Me 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Topic Page 
  • Read other SAP S/4HANA Cloud blog posts and follow

entry.title: Understanding OAuth 2.0 Behavior in SAP Datasphere’s Generic OData Connection
entry.content:
The Generic OData Connection in SAP Datasphere offers a standardized method for integrating any OData-compliant service. It enables seamless access to remote tables and supports data flows without requiring extensive configuration. However, due to gaps and ambiguities in the official documentation, this article aims to clarify some limitations and technical constraints that may impact implementation and operational stability when using OAuth 2.0 authentication.

Note: Recently, KBA 3586535 was published, briefly describing this issue and indicating that a fix will be available soon. This article aims to provide additional context to both the KBA and the official documentation, as especially the latter still contains some gaps.

SAP Datasphere supports multiple authentication types for the Generic OData connection: No Authentication, Username and Password, and OAuth 2.0, the latter being the default and most secure option. In this article, we focus on OAuth 2.0 with the Client Credentials grant type.

One configurable setting in this context is the OAuth Token Request Content Type, which determines the format in which token request parameters are sent to the authorization server (and the server needs to accept as he has to handle the request with this content type). SAP Datasphere provides two options:

  • URL Encoded (application/x-www-form-urlencoded)
  • JSON (application/json)

Now, let us consider a scenario where the target system (authorization server) expects a different content type, such as multipart/form-data. Since SAP Datasphere does not support this format natively, one might attempt to define it manually using custom HTTP headers.

At this point, a critical limitation becomes evident: although custom headers can be added to the OData connection, they do not override the internally managed OAuth Token Request Content Type. Even if a custom header explicitly sets Content-Type: multipart/form-data, the request will still be constructed and executed according to the selected value in the Datasphere configuration (e.g., application/json).

When tracing the communication between SAP Datasphere and the HANA OData Adapter (which is responsible for sending the token request to the authorization server), logs reveal entries such as:

extraHeaderParameters: Accept: */*;Content-Type: multipart/form-data;cache-control: no-cache
OAuth Token Request Content: application/json

Despite the presence of a custom Content-Type header, only the configured value (application/json) is actually used in the request. This behavior can only be confirmed through internal HANA OData Adapter logs, which are accessible solely by SAP Support.

This limitation is not clearly addressed in the official documentation. While SAP states that “security-relevant headers such as ‘authorization’ are not allowed,” it does not emphasize that this restriction implicitly includes Content-Type as well.

Credential Placement and OAuth Token Request Behavior

A second, and more critical, limitation concerns the placement of client credentials. Regardless of the content type selected, client_id and client_secret are never included in the body of the token request.

According to SAP's documentation, parameters should be included in the body based on the selected format (URL Encoded or JSON). However, this does not apply to client credentials. Log entries from the HANA OData Adapter show that only the grant_type is included in the request body.

2025-XX-XX 10:14:03.009360 D HttpClient WebRequestInternal.cpp(00XXX) : Sending the following body to server
CLOB START (Addr: 0x00007fexxxxxxx Len: 35)
0x00007fexxxxxxx {"grant_type": "client_credentials"}
CLOB END

At this point, debugging becomes challenging. SAP has confirmed that neither the client_id nor the client_secret are logged - not in the body, nor in the headers - due to security restrictions imposed by the HANA OData Adapter. This means there is no way to verify, via logs, whether client credentials were transmitted at all.

Through communication with SAP Support, we were able to clarify the system behavior: client credentials are always sent via the HTTP headers, never via the request body. If the target authorization server expects the credentials in the body the request will fail, as the body fields remain empty. This behavior is fixed and cannot be modified.

Implications for Integration Design

These two limitations, if unrecognized, can lead to difficult-to-diagnose errors during implementation:

  • Custom headers do not override the actual content type used in the token request body.
  • Client credentials are always transmitted via headers, regardless of the selected OAuth Token Request Content Type.

These nuances are not sufficiently addressed in the official documentation. In particular, statements such as:

"OAuth2 token request parameters will be in JSON format and included in the HTTP request body."

may lead to the incorrect assumption that client credentials are included in the body as well.

Alignment with RFC 6749: The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework

RFC 6749, Section 2.3.1, defines two valid methods for transmitting client credentials in the Client Credentials Grant flow:

  • HTTP Basic Authentication – where client_id and client_secret are passed in the Authorization header.
  • Request Body Parameters – where the credentials are included in the body using application/x-www-form-urlencoded.

SAP Datasphere's current implementation behaves in a somewhat rigid manner. While the system allows users to configure the OAuth Token Request Content Type as application/x-www-form-urlencoded, SAP Support has confirmed that the Generic OData Adapter transmits the client credentials exclusively via the HTTP header. They are never included in the request body, regardless of the configured content type.

This behavior appears to reflect the cautionary guidance in RFC 6749, which states:

"Including the client credentials in the request-body using the two parameters is NOT RECOMMENDED and SHOULD be limited to clients unable to directly utilize the HTTP Basic authentication scheme."
— RFC 6749, Section 2.3.1

In other words, when application/x-www-form-urlencoded is selected, it is standard and expected - according to the specification - that parameters such as client_id and client_secret be passed in the request body, unless the client is using HTTP Basic Authentication.

Therefore, SAP Datasphere’s behavior, while technically compliant with RFC 6749, lacks the flexibility explicitly allowed by the standard. It does not support a fallback to body-based credential transmission, which may lead to confusion or incompatibility with OAuth endpoints that expect credentials in the request body when this content type is used.

entry.title: Re: I'M unable to get the error message for this CRAP_WORKCENTER_CREATE
entry.content: Why are you using this function module? It's not released. What do you want to do exactly?

entry.title: Re: help me to find a BAPI/FM for to change the equipment BOM for ib02 t-code
entry.content: It's been already answered and solved here: https://www.google.com/search?q=bapi+iB02%20site:sap.com

entry.title: Accelerate Process Innovation with Enterprise Automation: SAP Signavio and SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
The ability to intelligently automate business processes is top of mind for many stakeholders involved in process management across organizations. According to a recent IDC study, annual spending on Intelligent Process Automation (IPA) technologies is projected to reach $102 billion by 2028. Of this, $19.9 billion will be allocated to AI-powered automation tools, with an impressive annual growth rate of 43.4%. 

While there will be certainly no shortage in resources to spend for automation software, the real challenge remains in the details: How can organizations implement a holistic approach for process excellence, which allows them to manage processes, identify bottlenecks, deliver scalable automations and integrate their process extension within a holistic process landscape. In another blogpost by Manuel,we have already outlined in detail how SAP’s framework on Enterprise Automation delivers a solution to the problem statement mentioned above. 

And this blogpost series will offer you a more detailed guidance on the interconnectivity of each service included in Enterprise Automation which are: 

  • SAP Build Process Automation 
  • SAP Integration Suite 
  • and of course SAP Signavio. 

Let’s explore the value you can unlock for your processes by combining SAP Signavio with SAP Integration Suite in this one in this one. You can refer the blog post to understand SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite use case pattern. Also, refer the blog post to understand SAP Signavio and SAP Build use case pattern.

 

What is Enterprise Automation? 

As outlined in previous blogpost Enterprise Automation with SAP: Enterprise automation with SAP software provides an integrated set of tools for process mining, automation, integration, monitoring, and optimization of your processes and workflows. It helps you quickly and intuitively gain insights into your business processes and identify the right automation tool for the right outcome. While each solution can be leveraged independently, Enterprise Automation offers native integration between SAP Build, SAP integration Suite and SAP Signavio – supplemented with best practices, guidance and pre-build content which can be implemented directly. 

 

 

 

 

  • SAP Signavio provides visibility into business processes and potential bottlenecks, root-cause analysis, and suggestions for improvement based on 200,000 industry benchmarks and best practices. This detailed understanding of inefficiencies across your organization enables you to optimize and automate processes. 

 

  • SAP Integration Suite is an integration platform as a service with thousands of prebuilt integrations, APIs, events and connectors to SAP and third-party software applications. The suite reduces complexity across on-premise and multi-cloud environments, helping you optimize comprehensive business processes. 

 

  • SAP Build to create automations and applications using drag-and-dropthe low-code and no-code design tools. Including pre-built automations and connectors for SAP software and third- party systems, the solutions let your business experts rapidly address process inefficiencies 

 

Here you can also see a visualization of the main components and its respective capabilities: 

 

 

Leverage the combined power of SAP Signavio and SAP Integration Suite 

 

1st scenario: Mine Third Party Data

 To identify inefficiencies and biggest automation potential in your processes, Signavio lets you consistently mine data within and across your IT software systems by leveraging the power of SAP Integration Suite.

You can leverage the broad range of connectors and adapters offered by SAP Integration Suite to seamlessly integrate with any business application by designing your own iflows or by leveraging  pre-built content.

 

In the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you’ll  find pre-built content connected to common third-party applications such as Salesforce, Coupa , Microsoft dynamics and many more to come.

Each integration flow serves the purpose of replicating business objects and their associated events from third-party systems into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

 The combined power of SAP Signavio and SAP Integration Suite helps you jump-start process mining initiatives and save time with ready-to-use content for multiple SAP and non SAP systems. You can now effectively prioritize your process mining efforts.

 Please refer the blogpost by Hoang on how to leverage pre-built content from non-SAP system like Salesforce.

If you would like to develop your own connectivity logic using Cloud Integration capability ,please refer the developer tutorial.

 

2nd  scenario: Connect mining to third party tools

 Another pattern to trigger processes without human intervention based on insights gained is using Cloud Integration capability.

Some of the scenarios in which the pattern could be leveraged be:

Automated Correction of Master Data:

 SAP Signavio Process Insights can identify issues such as incorrect or incomplete master data. Based on this insight, an integration flow in SAP Integration Suite could be triggered to automatically correct or enrich the master data in the relevant SAP or non SAP system

Dynamic Customer Notifications:

 If SAP Signavio Process Insights detects delays in the order-to-cash process, SAP Integration Suite could trigger notifications to customers regarding potential shipping delays or other relevant updates.

Automated Invoice Reconciliation: 

SAP Signavio Process Insights can identify discrepancies in invoice processing.This could trigger an integration flow in SAP Integration Suite to automatically reconcile invoices with corresponding purchase orders and goods receipts.

Proactive Inventory Management: 

When SAP Signavio Process Insights identifies potential stockouts or overstock situations, SAP Integration Suite could trigger an integration to adjust safety stock levels, initiate replenishment orders, or optimize warehouse operations.

Please refer documentation to understand how to configure cloud integration based actions.

 

 

3rd scenario: Event Driven Architectures

 To be able to continuously improve processes in real time, Process analyst can configure actions in SAP Signavio based on insights gained using process data.

Using Event Mesh capability of SAP Integration suite, any SAP or non-SAP processes can be triggered for improvement. Thereby, allowing the system to proactively identify issues and initiate corrective actions based on real-time monitoring of process performance. 

Examples of event-based process improvements triggered by SIGNAL queries (SAP Signavio Analytical Language) being run in SAP Signavio:

Alerting on order delays:

If a SIGNAL query detects that an order is taking significantly longer than usual to process, it could publish an event to trigger a workflow to the order management team to investigate and take corrective action in a non-SAP system

Escalating customer support issues:

When a SIGNAL query identifies a high volume of customer support tickets related to a specific product, it can automatically trigger a workflow in a non-SAP system by publishing an event to escalate the issue to a higher tier support team.

Dynamic resource allocation:

Based on real-time workload data from a SIGNAL query, an event can be published to trigger a non SAP process that could automatically reassign tasks to available team members to optimize capacity utilization. 

Please refer the help documentation as to how the event-based actions are configured by a Process Analyst in SAP Signavio.

 

Conclusion 

I hope this blog post has helped you identify the key synergies between SAP Signavio and SAP Integration Suite and how they can enable seamless automation across your enterprise. Please remember that the use cases provided are just examples and can be tailored and refined to meet your specific needs. 

For further guidance, I’ve included links to additional blog posts and resources on the Help Portal that will assist you in implementing these use cases. If you're interested in exploring more scenarios related to Enterprise Automation, feel free to check out the following links: 

 

  • Official Enterprise Automation with SAP page 
  • How to get started with Enterprise Automation Guide 
  • Whitepaper: Transition to SAP S/4HANA with Enterprise Automation 
  • Enterprise Automation with SAP Product Tour 
  • Enterprise Automation with SAP webinar

entry.title: Re: How the Upper limit and Lower limit of MR order are determined
entry.content:
Hi All,

Related to this, we were trying to figure out a solution to reduce the count of implausible reads by varying the percentage of deviation from the expected consumption. Before doing the same, I wanted to know if there is any standard table or method where these details are stored in SAP. Basically, i wanted to know if there is any way to fetch the expected consumption, actual consumption, expected lower limits and upper limits for multiple meter reads.

Thanks in advance for any lead related to this!

entry.title: Re: OUTB Table not found when printing Open Item Lists in...
entry.content: Hello, how did you solve this issue? The SAP note just explains they are working on a new patch.

entry.title: Re: IDOC Extension
entry.content: This doesn't really answer my 2 questions. Let's try differently. So, you have created 2 segment types and you added them as an extension to SHPMNT05. You are saying that 2 fields of these segments are filled, so it means that your custom ABAP code is filling these fields. If you can't debug it, maybe you are not activating the right options. Try activating the UPDATE DEBUGGING option.

entry.title: Re: How to create role Z and catalog Z in the Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi,

If you have created an APP, the way works for you to use the Z-Role to control the access of the APP.

Best Regards,

Zhehui

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) Q2 Update
entry.content:
Innovate, pilot or confirm the Enterprise Support portfolio content with our customers: SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) Q2 update!

Please find below our Q2/2025 ESAC workstreams update and an overview of all our workstreams running in Q2 here.  All major cloud solutions are covered.

ESAC is our innovation program that focuses on the development and improvement of the SAP Enterprise Support services portfolio (Foundational Support) with a selected group of customers.  Included in all cloud subscriptions, no additional fee!

We offer our customers the opportunity to be early adopters of latest SAP Enterprise Support innovations while also providing valuable support for their ongoing projects.

New workstreams for Q2:

  • Continuous Quality Check*: GoingLive Support for SAP Cloud Integration
  • Continuous Quality Check: Deployment Readiness for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  • Continuous Quality Check: Transformation Assessment for SAP IBP Tactical Supply Planning (Response and Supply or Sales and Operations)
  • Continuous Quality Check: Deployment Readiness for SAP Sales and Service Cloud, Version 2

New services on general availability (from Q1 workstreams):

  • Continuous Quality Check: Transformation Assessment for SAP Integrated Business Planning (live)
  • Continuous Quality Check: Deployment Readiness for SAP Fieldglass (April)
  • Continuous Quality Check: Transformation Assessment for Sustainability (April)

For more information, please refer to ESAC on the SAP Support Portal.

To  express interest or join the program: contact us via esac@sap.com.

Your SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council team

esac@sap.com

*Continuous Quality Check = remote service

entry.title: Re: operational reporting for procurement synchronous api
entry.content:
Hi @MOHAMMED_TAMBOLI 

the filter value "%7B%22createdDateFrom%22:%222025-01-01T00:00:00Z%22,%22createdDateTo%22:%222025-03-24T23:59:59Z%22%7D" is encoded already, the decoded version is "{"createdDateFrom":"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z","createdDateTo":"2025-03-24T23:59:59Z"}".

You can use post man to test this API, let me know if you need more information.

entry.title: Re: Why Are BRF+ Objects Inactive After Transporting from Development to Quality in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content: Hi, have you checked the transaction FDT_HELPERS? There are also a lot of notes (e.g. 2674741...) about BRF+ transport issues;

entry.title: Re: How to manually migrate House Banks during a system conversion using FCLM_BAM_MIGRATION
entry.content: Do i have to execute this step on every system or is it possible to transport the migrated house banks and house bank accounts?

entry.title: Re: AB08 Reversal Error: "Express document update was can...
entry.content:
hi

maybe there is no data for the asset in anlc table for 2025 year.

Regards Ryszard

entry.title: Joule in BDC
entry.content:
My question is to know when is going to be available Joule in BDC? is it going to be release by component? in that case, when it will be available in Datasphere, SAC... in each component?

Regards,

Julio,

entry.title: Re: NOW RELEASED: Upload and work with external files in SAP Cloud ALM for implementation
entry.content: @MULYS001 & @Cocquerel : as of now only BTP DMS repository works.

entry.title: Re: Event based revenue recognition -project based sales
entry.content:
Hi @Nikhil1905 ,

Currently, the configuration doesn’t allow for different revenue recognition keys for the same item category. However, you can further define the revenue recognition key at the material level within the SSCUI for the same item category. For example, you can set up a cost-based POC key for material A under item category TAN, and a completed contract method key for material B for the same item category. Another workaround in the current design is to define one revenue recognition key as the default, such as the cost-based POC key, since it covers most scenarios. You can then change the key to the completed contract method individually at WBS billing element level in the Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Projects app using the "Change Revenue Recognition Key" feature based on manual decisions. Regarding your second question, when a revenue recognition key is defined, your shall ensure that the derived recognition key matches the existing recognition key to proceed with project account assignment to a sales order item. Additionally, for manual decisions on the revenue recognition key at the project/WBS billing element level, there is a CIP: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/339380.  If your customer wishes for the same, please encourage them to vote for it to support its inclusion in the future roadmap. 

With best regards, Chenchu

entry.title: Re: Exchange of Story Reports among companies that work with Stories already
entry.content: Hi Mathias, The idea is not bad but you need to take into account that many of us here are consultants who work for different customers and we've invested a lot of time into developing of our best practices Story reports. For instance, we even sell our own developed Stories as "plag&play" solution for the different clients. I don't think it's feasible to share all the reports easily, however, I would rather do sharing of some specific solutions/approaches/formulas instead.

entry.title: Re: New Review Order Progress - Multilevel View App
entry.content: Hi @Markus_Oertelt . Great App. Will be part of my demos from now on. Appreciate.

entry.title: Re: Cancel picking of EWM Production Material Request
entry.content: Hi Ajit, we follow this way that is quite easy when production is completed: what is left on PSA is what it was not used. On the other hand when production is not completed and the whole PMR is cancelled: we need to execute all manually for each staged material. It sounds strange that there is not such a ready-to-use app as CANCPICK is for the Outbound Delivery process

entry.title: Re: SAC chart variance - "The time dimension does not have a valid hierarchy." error
entry.content:
Hello,

Did you found any solution for this problem ? If yes, can you please share, I am facing the same issue

thanks and regards

entry.title: Re: New Review Order Progress - Multilevel View App
entry.content: Hi @bernhard_barth - thanks a lot for your feedback and good system demonstrations! Hopefully we can enhance the app soon as well.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Application Studio access permission error
entry.content: it worked for me also .Thanks

entry.title: Flagging Materials in SAP for New Customs Certificate Requirements
entry.content:
Hi,

The user reports that new customs regulations require certificates to be included with shipments. They have prepared an Excel sheet listing the materials and their corresponding HTS (Harmonized Tariff Schedule) codes that fall under the new requirements. They want these materials to be marked in SAP (e.g., with a flag, status, or another method) to ensure that the complete documentation is included with each shipment.

Do you have guys any idea?

Thank you in advance for any help!

BR

entry.title: android.permission.READ_MEDIA_IMAGES and android.permission.READ_MEDIA_VIDEO
entry.content:
Hey everyone,

I've been trying to publish an update for my app on the Google Play Store for a while now, but I keep getting an error asking for an explanation about why I’m using these two permissions:

- android.permission.READ_MEDIA_IMAGES

- android.permission.READ_MEDIA_VIDEO

The issue is that my app isn’t supposed to access photos or videos on the device—I only use Set and Get item from storage. Do you think these permissions could be related to that?

Also, is there a way to check which permissions the app uses before compiling it?

Thanks!

(I'm using v.4.12.218)

entry.title: I am getting privilege error while providing access to the schema for the role."
entry.content:
Hello

Reference tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hana-cloud-mission-trial-6.html

I got the below error while performing above tutorial

I have created the HDI manually in the cockpit, sharing snaps below


I tried giving privilege from Grant HDI container privilege option present in BAS , but it is not giving grantable to others privilege.

Can someone help to find solution for the issue?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP GROUP REPORTING CONFIGURATION STEPS
entry.content: Hi Experts , from where i can get documentation for group reporting all steps of configuration with screenshots and process workflow , i am new to the field

entry.title: Re: PS Easy Cost Planning Integration with S/4HANA Financial Planning
entry.content: Does the function copy actual to plan also update the ACDOCP table ?

entry.title: Re: AB08 Reversal Error: "Express document update was can...
entry.content: Did you execute SM13 to identify the exact (update task) FM triggering the Abort Message (A dump is not always generated)  and sometimes the initial error message?

entry.title: OUTB Table not found when printing Open Item Lists in Sales
entry.content: Hello everyone, I have SAP Business One Hana, and when opening the list of open items in the sales module, I get the error "OUTB Table not found...". I have read some posts, and they recommend checking SAP Note 3287549. Has anyone managed to solve this problem without upgrading the version?

entry.title: Re: COMMIT/ROLLBACK in Custom Action in SAP RAP
entry.content: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/rap-transactional-model-and-sap-luw

entry.title: Re: Event based revenue recognition -project based sales
entry.content: Thanks @Chenchu , for your prompt reply. This helps a lot!

entry.title: Re: ABAP - Creation of Warehouse Task with inbound delivery
entry.content:
Hi,

Like Ajit is saying. If you want to use customized logic I would still suggest to use class services instead of FM. The service help you to perform the steps outside the standard. Because you are trying to perform the actions with FM I think that is what you want to do. If so have a look at /scwm/cl_dlv_management_prd. For WT creation I would suggest same as Ajit, look into PPF actions.

To be honest it is still a bit unclear what the actual goal is.

Regards Mark

entry.title: SAP GROUP REPORTING LEARNING
entry.content: Hi Found this link  for group reporting learning , where i can get the full content for S4 GROUP REPORTING FOR ALLTHE PROCESS FROM A TO Z  , please advise with thanks

entry.title: Re: Announcing the New "In-Metro" Disaster Recovery Solution for SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi NiklasSiemer

I want to ask some questions about Integration Suite DR, including information about In-metro backup?
How to backup if there are any, and what methods are used?

 

BR，

Rika

entry.title: Re: Reactive maintenance order w.r.t notification error (Work center does not exist)
entry.content: x

entry.title: Re: Adapt Fiori My inbox app and replace TASKPROCESSING oData with custom oData.
entry.content: Have you solved it. Please share it with us if solved

entry.title: Re: Flexible workflow PR approval with HR mini master
entry.content:
Hi 

You might have to look into teams and responsibility, there you can create teams and delegate the approval based on material groups.

Else you have to add the material group as a trigger in your workflow
